truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 8 of 20 true vulnerabilities with exact code evidence but missed 12. Several findings contained severity and CWE misclassifications that inaccurately reflected the true risk profile. No hallucinated code or unsupported findings were present in the previous output.

Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format string injection vulnerability using user-controlled input in a printf call.
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow risk in the factorial computation loop.
Run 2 missed the use-after-free vulnerability involving stage_deferred_history_flush and memory pool cleanup.
Run 2 correctly flagged command injection in the config validator via an unvalidated system() call.
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in input processing.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly noted the race condition caused by reading the counter value before acquiring the lock.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; session_token_ = "EMERGENCY_SESSION"; append_override_audit_event(); return true; }
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the logic bomb/emergency override backdoor in the authentication flow.
Run 2 did not flag the path traversal vulnerability in configuration loading.
Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in the expression parser.
Run 2 correctly flagged the hardcoded administrator credentials.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the weak PRNG but misclassified the CWE and severity.
Run 2 missed the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging.
Run 2 missed the insecure temporary file handling vulnerability.
Run 2 missed the persistent authentication state issue.
Run 2 missed the unhandled exception risk in variable parsing.
Run 2 missed the integer underflow in memory-pool block splitting.
Run 2 missed the unbounded recursion risk in the parser.
Run 2 missed the data race in the result cache.
Run 2 missed the TOCTOU vulnerability in configuration loading.
Run 2 missed the integer division by zero risk in command routing.
